source_file: 4-5.FL.2 Rubric for Handout B. SMART Goal Brainstorm and Planning.pdf
output_file: 4-5.FL.2 Rubric for Handout B. SMART Goal Brainstorm and Planning.md

analysis.summary: This is an assessment rubric for evaluating student performance on a SMART goal brainstorm and planning activity in a grades 4-5 financial literacy lesson. The rubric assesses five criteria (Goals, Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-based) across four performance levels (Excellent, Satisfactory, Needs Improvement, Incomplete).

entities.name: Grades 4-5 Financial Literacy Lesson 2
entities.description: A financial literacy lesson for elementary students focusing on goal setting

entities.name: Handout B: SMART Goal Brainstorm and Planning
entities.description: A student handout worksheet for brainstorming and planning SMART goals

entities.name: SMART Goal Framework
entities.description: A goal-setting methodology using Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-based criteria

entities.name: Goal Clarity Criteria
entities.description: Assessment criterion evaluating how clearly students state their goals

entities.name: Specific Criteria
entities.description: Assessment criterion evaluating whether goals are clearly defined with specific details

entities.name: Measurable Criteria
entities.description: Assessment criterion evaluating whether goals include clear criteria for measuring progress

entities.name: Achievable Criteria
entities.description: Assessment criterion evaluating whether goals are realistic and attainable

entities.name: Relevant Criteria
entities.description: Assessment criterion evaluating whether goals are relevant and meaningful to the student

entities.name: Time-based Criteria
entities.description: Assessment criterion evaluating whether goals include a clear timeframe for completion

entities.name: Four-Point Performance Scale
entities.description: Performance levels: Excellent, Satisfactory, Needs Improvement, Incomplete

relationships.source: Handout B: SMART Goal Brainstorm and Planning

relationships.context: The rubric criterion evaluates student performance on the handout activity
Specific is one of the five components of the SMART goal framework
Measurable is one of the five components of the SMART goal framework
Achievable is one of the five components of the SMART goal framework
Relevant is one of the five components of the SMART goal framework
Time-based is one of the five components of the SMART goal framework
The lesson teaches students about the SMART goal framework
Each assessment criterion is evaluated using the four-point performance scale
